title: Curated PR 2692 lesson for src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c
text: Melee decomp: SysDolphin baselib matches PR #2692 performed implementation-only SysDolphin baselib matching work in audio, camera object, particle, and TExp files. The PR body reports 12 newly exact functions across 4 files with zero slice regressions, while the bot report shows 11 new matches and 7 unmatched-item improvements. Review focused on avoiding fakematching: pointer-math/while-loop shapes in axdriver, local externs used to influence cobj inlining, new pragmas in particle, static-vs-extern rodata placement, and a likely fsqrt inline in particle. src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c Decomp lessons: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Matching tactics: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Naming: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Review: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Terms: pr-2692; SysDolphin baselib matches; axdriver.c; cobj.c; particle.c; texpdag.c; AXDriver_8038C6C0; AXDriver_8038DA70; HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize; AXDriver_803B95F8; AXDriver_803B9618; RevHiDims; revstd_dims; HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtr; HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr; HSD_CObjGetUpVector; get_up_vector_for_viewing_mtx; HSD_TExpSimplify2; hsd_803931A4; hsd_80394068; hsd_80395644; hsd_80395D88; hsd_80398F8C; pool_data off; PAD_STACK; fsqrt inline; __frsqrte; CLAMP cmd_val; pointer math; fakematching; natural loops; local extern; inline helper layers

payload.title: Melee decomp: SysDolphin baselib matches
payload.key_file_role: Particle matching work; introduced ParticleFloatBytes, adjusted hsd_804CF740 declaration/definition placement, changed hsd_803931A4 channel flag access, added pool_data pragma around hsd_80394068, improved screen-state and exception-node access patterns, and modified math in hsd_80398F8C.
payload.summary: PR #2692 performed implementation-only SysDolphin baselib matching work in audio, camera object, particle, and TExp files. The PR body reports 12 newly exact functions across 4 files with zero slice regressions, while the bot report shows 11 new matches and 7 unmatched-item improvements. Review focused on avoiding fakematching: pointer-math/while-loop shapes in axdriver, local externs used to influence cobj inlining, new pragmas in particle, static-vs-extern rodata placement, and a likely fsqrt inline in particle.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a local extern declaration is used only to alter MWCC inlining, reviewers may reject it even if it improves objdiff; try narrow static inline helper layers first.
evidence_refs: review_comments: author claimed local extern improved HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtr/HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr objdiff
review_comments: jurrejelle r3409828143 called it a regression and suggested inline helper layers

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewer pressure favored natural loops and less pointer math in AXDriver_8038DA70; pointer-table relocation code should still be tested against cleaner for-loop or typed access shapes before accepting generated-looking while/index juggling.
New pragmas can be controversial even when narrowly scoped and match-improving; reviewers may still object that the shape is not likely authored source.
evidence_refs: review_comments: author r3409741017 reports pool_data wrapper gives hsd_80393DA0 100%
review_comments: jurrejelle r3409832861 says the pragma is wrong and should eventually be removed

decomp_lessons.lesson: For hand-written square-root approximations using __frsqrte refinement, search for existing fsqrt inline patterns before preserving expanded generated math.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use bounded for loops over repeated dimension groups in HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize rather than repeated manual summation blocks.
Introduce small inline wrapper layers to influence whether HSD_CObjGetUpVector is inlined at viewing-matrix call sites.
Keep PAD_STACK narrow and function-local where used in cobj and particle matching changes; evidence shows PAD_STACK(24), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK adjustments in touched functions.
A narrow #pragma pool_data off inside an existing push/dont_inline block was used for hsd_80394068, but review evidence is mixed and warns against treating this as normal style.
For float absolute value and square-root-like code in particle, raw bit-masking and __frsqrte refinement appeared in the diff; reviewer suggested searching for an existing fsqrt inline instead.

naming_conventions.convention: Small static inline helpers used with local semantic names for camera object field access.

naming_conventions.name: AXDriver_803B95F8 / AXDriver_803B9618
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style data names appear in the review discussion for reverb dimension data; reviewer feedback pushed against inappropriate extern conversion and toward static storage in source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static const AXDriver_803B95F8 and AXDriver_803B9618 inside HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged a possible extern issue around replacing static reverb dimension data with extern AXDriver_803B95F8.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged new pragmas; later argued the wrapper was tightly scoped and objdiff-supported for hsd_80393DA0.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged new local externs; later argued they were local matching evidence for HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtr and HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Called much of AXDriver_8038DA70 fakematching and asked to try reducing pointer math and turning whiles into fors.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Said the reverb dimension extern conversion was wrong and should be turned back into statics.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Rejected local externs as an inlining-control tactic and suggested adding inline helper layers instead.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Objected to adding pragmas as not likely original authored style even if behavior is unchanged.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested hsd_80398F8C probably contains a call to an existing fsqrt inline pattern.

title: Curated PR 2694 lesson for tools/check/tests/jobj_flags.py
text: tools/check: add jobj-flags --fix PR 2694 added a local --fix mode to tools/check, with dry-run unified diff support, and made only the jobj-flags check auto-fix raw hidden-flag literals to JOBJ_HIDDEN. assert-macros remained detection-only because the PR body and comments say that conversion requires judgement. Tests were added for jobj flag fixing behavior, including preserving surrounding text, non-match safety, and idempotence. tools/check/tests/jobj_flags.py Decomp lessons: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Matching tactics: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Naming: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Review: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Terms: PR 2694; tools/check --fix; tools/check --dry-run; jobj-flags; fix_jobj_flags; scan_jobj_flags; JOBJ_HIDDEN; raw 0x10 literal; HSD_JObjSetFlags; HSD_JObjClearFlags; HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll; HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll; assert-macros detection-only; __assert; HSD_ASSERT; HSD_ASSERTMSG; HSD_ASSERTREPORT; clang-query; clang-tidy; parser combinators; difflib.unified_diff; strip_noise preserves offsets

payload.title: tools/check: add jobj-flags --fix
payload.key_file_role: Adds unit tests for fix_jobj_flags replacement forms, exact text preservation, non-match behavior, and idempotence.
payload.summary: PR 2694 added a local --fix mode to tools/check, with dry-run unified diff support, and made only the jobj-flags check auto-fix raw hidden-flag literals to JOBJ_HIDDEN. assert-macros remained detection-only because the PR body and comments say that conversion requires judgement. Tests were added for jobj flag fixing behavior, including preserving surrounding text, non-match safety, and idempotence.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Automated cleanup is safest when limited to exact, token-only decomp style fixes; judgement-heavy rewrites should remain detection-only or manual.
evidence_refs: pr_body: raw hidden-flag literal replaced with JOBJ_HIDDEN; purely mechanical and exact
diff: fixes change only tokens, not layout

decomp_lessons.lesson: For raw JObj hidden flag literals, the intended cleanup target in this PR is JOBJ_HIDDEN, but only when the hidden flag appears as the complete argument to supported HSD_JObj flag calls.
evidence_refs: diff: _HIDDEN_LITERAL regex accepts 16, 0x10, octal-like 020 forms with suffixes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert macro conversion may require negating an if-guard and checking text equivalence; this PR treats that as not safely auto-fixable.
evidence_refs: diff comment: Detection only; if-guard must be negated and matched against assert text

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a project check/fixer for mechanical JObj hidden flag cleanup instead of hand-editing many raw 0x10 literal sites.
evidence_refs: pr_body: mechanical fix can be applied directly instead of by hand
pr_body: Replay reproduced #2675 across 73 files with zero content differences except clang-format line wrapping

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: After using the fixer, run clang-format because the PR claims fixes change only tokens, not layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Fixes change only tokens, not layout — run clang-format afterward
diff docstring: Fixes change only tokens, not layout, so run clang-format afterward

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use --fix --dry-run to inspect the exact unified diff before writing changes.
evidence_refs: pr_body: --fix --dry-run prints a unified diff and writes nothing

naming_conventions.convention: tools/check check identifiers use kebab-case such as jobj-flags and assert-macros; CLI disables are derived as --no-<check>.
evidence_refs: diff: Check.name comment says kebab-case id drives --no-<name>

naming_conventions.convention: Scanner and fixer functions follow scan_<check> and fix_<check> naming, as shown by scan_jobj_flags and fix_jobj_flags.
Private parsing helpers in tools/check use leading underscores, e.g. _jobj_literal_spans, _assert_sites, _has_todo, and _assert_first_arg.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the maintainability of procedural parsing and asked whether pure regex or parser combinators could be used.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that regex likely cannot capture all assert cases in the project; mentioned considering clang-query and parser combinators, with parser combinators implying an extra Python dependency.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Tested clang-query and clang-tidy locally and asked whether checks should live alongside tools/issues or as standalone clang-query matchers in CI.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Approved but stated the main issue was that the implementation seemed annoying to maintain as written and preferred one of the alternatives discussed earlier.

title: Curated PR 2695 lesson for src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c
text: grbigblueroute: match C85C, DED4, DF80 sizes; improve CD20 and fix sdata2 ordering Closed, unmerged PR touching only src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c. It attempted stage decomp matching improvements for grBigBlueRoute functions C85C, CD20, DED4, and DF80, plus .sdata2 constant-order work. The PR body claimed exact size matches for C85C, DED4, and DF80 and a 4-byte CD20 gap, but the author immediately commented that an agent made the PR before it was ready, so claimed results should be treated as provisional rather than accepted. src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c Decomp lessons: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Matching tactics: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Naming: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Review: [object Object] Terms: pr-2695; grbigblueroute; src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c; grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C; grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20; grBigBlueRoute_8020DED4; grBigBlueRoute_8020DF80; C85C; CD20; DED4; DF80; sdata2 ordering; Ground_801C0498; Ground_801C38BC; stmw r24; stmw r25; GPR spills; FPR spills f16-f31; PAD_STACK; volatile Vec3 rot; RE_ENTRY; decomp-permuter; ternary if-else; MWCC register pressure; not ready agent PR

payload.title: grbigblueroute: match C85C, DED4, DF80 sizes; improve CD20 and fix sdata2 ordering
payload.key_file_role: Only changed source file; contains all function-size, control-flow, register-pressure, and .sdata2-ordering edits in the PR.
payload.summary: Closed, unmerged PR touching only src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c. It attempted stage decomp matching improvements for grBigBlueRoute functions C85C, CD20, DED4, and DF80, plus .sdata2 constant-order work. The PR body claimed exact size matches for C85C, DED4, and DF80 and a 4-byte CD20 gap, but the author immediately commented that an agent made the PR before it was ready, so claimed results should be treated as provisional rather than accepted.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a near-match differs by a small prologue/register-save gap, record the exact register-save difference, stack frame size, FPR spill range, and tested failed tactics rather than presenting the function as fully matched.
Equivalent source-level control-flow forms can affect target size and branch shape; this PR tried comparison inversion for C85C and if/else instead of ternary for CD20.

Constant ordering work in a code-matching PR is risky when it depends on discarded computations; this PR is evidence of the tactic being attempted, not evidence that the tactic should be accepted as style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Comparison inversion for branch/size shape.
Declaration/local-order adjustments for register pressure and codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: CD20 moved `root_jobj`, `jobj`, and `i` declarations after Vec3 locals. The PR body says declaration-order tests failed to close the 4-byte GPR gap.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Known gap says declaration order tests failed

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move computations earlier to affect live ranges/control flow.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: CD20 moved spline/tangent/rotation computations before xC adjustment in case 0.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Ternary-to-if/else conversion.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: CD20 changed the jobj update at loop end from a conditional expression to explicit if/else, with the PR body saying this matched target control flow.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Discarded expression to perturb .sdata2 constant ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR inserted `45.0f * Ground_801C0498();` and states it was intended to fix .sdata2 ordering without a variable live range. Because the PR was not ready, this should remain a reviewed tactic candidate, not a style precedent.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Document failed search space.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR body reports declaration order, block scoping, volatile variations, ternary vs if-else patterns, and 90k+ decomp-permuter iterations did not reduce CD20 register pressure.

naming_conventions.text: The diff retains address-style function names such as grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20, grBigBlueRoute_8020DED4, and grBigBlueRoute_8020DF80; no new semantic global renames are evidenced.
Local names are role-based and short where visible: `gp`, `gobj`, `jobj`, `root_jobj`, `fighter`, `fighter_pos`, `pos`, `rot`, `p0`, `p1`, `tangent`, `road_tan`, and `road`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 declaration and local blocks

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses `RE_ENTRY` as a macro alias for the indexed route entry: `#define RE_ENTRY (&((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i])`; this is existing context in the diff, not evidence of a new global naming standard.

review_feedback.author: kolt-mcb
review_feedback.text: sorry my agent made this pr before it was ready.

title: Curated PR 2697 lesson for src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c
text: grbigblueroute: match grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 Matched stage function grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 by changing its return type from void to f32, preserving the magnitude returned by lbVector_Normalize, returning it at function end, and updating the owning header declaration. payload.title: grbigblueroute: match grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64
payload.key_file_role: Function definition updated: grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 now returns f32, stores lbVector_Normalize result in mag, and returns it after existing Vec3 clamping/zeroing logic.
payload.summary: Matched stage function grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 by changing its return type from void to f32, preserving the magnitude returned by lbVector_Normalize, returning it at function end, and updating the owning header declaration. Bot report showed one new match for main/melee/gr/grbigblueroute with grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 moving from 98.60% to 100.00%.

decomp_lessons.text: When a normalized vector helper returns a magnitude, check whether the caller's original function also returns that magnitude; a previously void decompilation may be discarding the value needed for a match.
After recovering a non-void return type in a function body, update the owning header declaration in the same focused change.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use the callee return value directly as a local, then return it at the end, when matching evidence indicates the original function propagates a helper's return value.
Signature/prototype correction can be the matching tactic: here the key change was void -> f32 for grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64, with no data, pragma, or inline-assembly edits shown.

naming_conventions.text: The recovered lbVector_Normalize return value was named mag, a concise role name for magnitude.

title: Curated PR 2698 lesson for reqs/build.txt
text: Bump Python requirements Tooling-only PR that refreshed generated Python requirement lock files under reqs/. The PR body says to run `pip-sync reqs/*.txt` locally, notes that `pip-sync` comes from `pip install pip-tools`, and gives a fallback command to force-reinstall `pip==26.0` if the operation fails. The diffs update pip-compile headers to Python 3.12 and explicit `--output-file=... --strip-extras` commands, and remove `pycparser==2.21` from `reqs/decomp.txt`. No decomp source, naming, assembly, or review discussion is present in the slice. reqs/build.txt Terms: PR 2698; Bump Python requirements; ribbanya; reqs/build.txt; reqs/decomp.txt; reqs/dev.txt; reqs/diff.txt; reqs/gen-pages.txt; reqs/misc.txt; pip-sync reqs/*.txt; pip-tools; pip-compile; --strip-extras; --output-file; Python 3.12; Python 3.11; pycparser==2.21; m2c requirements; pip==26.0

payload.summary: Tooling-only PR that refreshed generated Python requirement lock files under reqs/. The PR body says to run `pip-sync reqs/*.txt` locally, notes that `pip-sync` comes from `pip install pip-tools`, and gives a fallback command to force-reinstall `pip==26.0` if the operation fails. The diffs update pip-compile headers to Python 3.12 and explicit `--output-file=... --strip-extras` commands, and remove `pycparser==2.21` from `reqs/decomp.txt`. No decomp source, naming, assembly, or review discussion is present in the slice.

title: Curated PR 2700 lesson for config/GALE01/symbols.txt
text: link ftaction PR 2700 linked `melee/ft/ftaction.c` by changing it from NonMatching to Matching, with small code-shape adjustments in ftaction command handlers, bitfield naming/layout updates for `footstep_fx_0`, and symbol metadata changes for local jump tables and ftaction sdata2 constants. The bot report records 3 new matches, including `ftAction_80072CD8`, `ftAction_80072E4C`, and `.sdata2` for `main/melee/ft/ftaction`. config/GALE01/symbols.txt Decomp lessons: [object Object]; [object Object] Matching tactics: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object] Naming: [object Object]; [object Object] Review: [object Object] Terms: pr-2700; link ftaction; jurrejelle; ftaction.c Matching; main/melee/ft/ftaction; ftAction_8007121C; ftAction_80072CD8; ftAction_80072E4C; ft_80084BFC; ftAction_80071B50; SKIP_CMD; NEXT_CMD; GET_FIGHTER; gobj->user_data; cmd_words; footstep_fx_0; boneId; symbols.txt; sdata2; jtbl_803C08A4; @576; @243

payload.title: link ftaction
payload.key_file_role: Symbol metadata update for ftaction data/jump table and sdata2 constants, changing generated global names to local numeric labels.
payload.summary: PR 2700 linked `melee/ft/ftaction.c` by changing it from NonMatching to Matching, with small code-shape adjustments in ftaction command handlers, bitfield naming/layout updates for `footstep_fx_0`, and symbol metadata changes for local jump tables and ftaction sdata2 constants. The bot report records 3 new matches, including `ftAction_80072CD8`, `ftAction_80072E4C`, and `.sdata2` for `main/melee/ft/ftaction`.

decomp_lessons.text: A translation unit link-up may require coordinated source, symbol metadata, and build-config edits; here `ftaction.c` was only marked Matching after source tweaks and local symbol-label updates landed together.

For MWCC matching in this slice, source-level helper/macros were not always preserved: `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` was replaced by direct `gobj->user_data`, and `SKIP_CMD(cmd, 3)` was expanded into three increments in two ftaction handlers. Treat this as local matching evidence, not a global style preference.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used an exact decimal float literal `0.003906f` instead of `(1 / 256.0f)` in hitbox scaling and offset calculations.
Adjusted stack/local layout in `ftAction_80072CD8` by reordering locals, replacing an anonymous three-word struct with `u32 cmd_words[3]`, and adding an `unused` local.
Expanded `SKIP_CMD(cmd, 3)` to three explicit `++(cmd)->u` statements in `ftAction_80072CD8` and `ftAction_80072E4C`.
Changed direct fighter access from `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` to `gobj->user_data` in two handlers, apparently as part of the local codegen match.
Changed ftaction `.sdata2` and jump-table symbol names/scopes to local numeric labels, matching the linked-unit metadata used by this PR.

naming_conventions.text: `footstep_fx_0` uses `boneId` as a semantic bitfield name for the 8-bit field immediately after the 6-bit opcode; adjacent uncertain fields remain address/bit-style such as `x1_b7`, `x2_b0_7`, and `x3_b0_7`.
Local ftaction data labels in symbols metadata may use numeric local-label style such as `@576` or `@243` rather than invented `ftAction_...` globals when the diff marks them as local scope.

decomp_lessons.text: A translation unit link-up may require coordinated source, symbol metadata, and build-config edits; here `ftaction.c` was only marked Matching after source tweaks and local symbol-label updates landed together.

For MWCC matching in this slice, source-level helper/macros were not always preserved: `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` was replaced by direct `gobj->user_data`, and `SKIP_CMD(cmd, 3)` was expanded into three increments in two ftaction handlers. assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used an exact decimal float literal `0.003906f` instead of `(1 / 256.0f)` in hitbox scaling and offset calculations.
Adjusted stack/local layout in `ftAction_80072CD8` by reordering locals, replacing an anonymous three-word struct with `u32 cmd_words[3]`, and adding an `unused` local.
Expanded `SKIP_CMD(cmd, 3)` to three explicit `++(cmd)->u` statements in `ftAction_80072CD8` and `ftAction_80072E4C`.
Changed direct fighter access from `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` to `gobj->user_data` in two handlers, apparently as part of the local codegen match.
Changed ftaction `.sdata2` and jump-table symbol names/scopes to local numeric labels, matching the linked-unit metadata used by this PR.

naming_conventions.text: `footstep_fx_0` uses `boneId` as a semantic bitfield name for the 8-bit field immediately after the 6-bit opcode; adjacent uncertain fields remain address/bit-style such as `x1_b7`, `x2_b0_7`, and `x3_b0_7`.

The target now uses ft_80084C74's four returned terrain SFX ids, keeps the common DownBound SFX table pointer live, splits sqrt/weight multiplication, reorders Vec3 zero stores, and narrows the stack pad to PAD_STACK(8). Measured target movement improved from the local baseline 82.00714% to 99.84286%. The only remaining code diff is the inlined sqrtf volatile temp stack slot: target stores/loads r1+0x14 while current source stores/loads r1+0x0c. ft_80084C74 is the arg0 == 2 wrapper over ft_80084A80, and for arg0 == 2 the first pointer receives four SFX ids. The first SFX choice in ftCo_800976A4 uses the terrain-provided sfx_ids[4], while the later SFX choice uses ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds. Keeping a local common_sfx_ids pointer matches the target's early lis/addi into r31 and later indexed loads from r31. The target zeroes vec1/vec0 fields in z, y, x order at stack offsets 0x2c/0x20, 0x28/0x1c, then 0x24/0x18. The final remaining target mismatch is only the inlined sqrtf volatile temporary slot: current MWCC emits r1+0x0c while the target uses r1+0x14.

title: Worker progress for order_dag
text: src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c main/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag order_dag progress Improved order_dag from 80.42466% to 99.52055%. The retained source replaces generated-looking manual copy/unroll code with the natural copy loop, mutates done_set/ready_set/depth in place to match the target prologue shape, removes the now-unneeded PAD_STACK, and separates full dependency accumulation. The remaining diff is a small register-allocation permutation in the full-dependency accumulation loop. The original-looking source for the min_order copy is the simple natural loop `for (i = 0; i < num; i++) { min_order[i] = order[i]; }`; MWCC expands it to the target unrolled copy and remainder loops. The target prologue is better modeled by mutating `depth`, `done_set`, and `ready_set` in place (`order[depth++]`, `done_set |= 1 << idx`, `ready_set &= ~(1 << idx)`) than by separate new_scheduled/new_available locals. The remaining order_dag mismatch is isolated to register allocation in the full-dependency accumulation loop; instruction selection and control flow are otherwise very close. HSD_TExpSchedule's 99.89510% checkdiff result is not introduced by these edits; the saved pre-edit direct-compile object had the same HSD_TExpSchedule score.

title: Worker progress for ifStock_802F89F8
text: src/melee/if/ifstock.c main/melee/if/ifstock ifStock_802F89F8 progress Improved ifStock_802F89F8 from 75.89496% to 87.38655% by recovering a more original-looking digit/count lowering shape: split the digit update loop into a static inline boundary, restored the extra divisor loop/lifetime before digit JObj flag clearing, rewrote digit temp handling, and reshaped the coin digit-count loop into the 32-iteration/goto form that matches the original unrolled mtctr loop much more closely. The original ifStock_802F89F8 digit-count loop is a 32-step divide-by-10 loop unrolled in groups of eight with mtctr=4; using i as the temporary counter and assigning count after the loop matches that structure far better than the previous 9-iteration count++ loop. The digit update body is sensitive to inline/lifetime boundaries; extracting it into a static inline and adding the pre-clear divisor loop improved the target substantially. Remaining mismatch is now concentrated in early register allocation/global-base register choice and in the special-case coins % 10 computation, where the candidate fully hoists the modulo result but the original only hoists part of the magic-division sequence.

Remaining exact-match blockers are not semantic unknowns but compiler-shape issues: current object saves r21-r31 with a smaller frame under the retained best score, while target saves r17-r31 with a 0x98 frame; several surviving mismatches are register-allocation and branch-address fallout. A source_permuter replay found a higher non-retained candidate using #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off plus an artificial Vec3 pad, indicating codegen is sensitive to optimizer/register pressure, but that candidate was rejected as non-reviewable.

Unused decompiler locals can affect MWCC frame/register allocation in this function; removing the unused EOB locals gave a measurable score improvement.

text: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage ftCo_8008EB58 progress Retained a reviewable rewrite of ftCo_8008EB58 that raises the target from the observed 38.79487% checkdiff baseline to 98.52564%. The remaining target mismatch is isolated to initial fp load register allocation: candidate uses r3 then moves to r31; target loads directly into r31.

title: Worker progress for lbRefract_PObjLoad
text: src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c main/melee/lb/lbrefract lbRefract_PObjLoad progress Improved lbRefract_PObjLoad from the leased baseline 70.48193% to 86.85542%. The retained edit reshapes the vertex descriptor stride scan and display-list copy loop toward the m2c/assembly evidence: attr is captured in the loop condition, range dispatch is laid out with shared labels and attr_type switches, command-byte consumption uses display[offset++], the 16-bit count expression is shaped for bit insertion, and the unrolled-copy trip count uses an unsigned shifted iterator. Remaining mismatches are mostly register allocation/lifetime: stride/last_offset still allocate as r6/r5 instead of target r5/r6, and the display-list loop keeps offset/count in r7/r6 instead of target r10/r12. src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c had pre-existing dirty hunks outside the leased target, including lbl_804D63E8/skip8_804D63E8 and lbl_804336D0.unk_8 typing changes; these were preserved and not reverted. The target assembly wants the vertex descriptor attr loaded in the loop condition and reused in the loop body; adding a GXAttr attr local assigned in the while condition improved matching. The color attribute stride path matches a switch over GX_INDEX16, GX_INDEX8, and default comp_type handling better than an attr_type >= GX_INDEX8 ladder. The display-list command byte is better represented as display[offset++] in the branch condition; this restored the target addi-before-branch shape. Hoisting n = count - 8 before the count > 8 branch and using a u32 shifted iterator improved the unrolled-copy loop shape. Remaining high-value mismatch is register allocation/lifetime: target uses stride/last_offset as r5/r6 and display offset/count as r10/r12, while current code still tends toward r6/r5 and r7/r6.

The remaining early mismatches are dominated by register allocation choices for line_base/j0/j1 and an inserted move; later mismatches include scheduling around groundCollVtx and floating constants in the vertex comparison loop. title: Worker progress for fn_803ACFC0
text: src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7 fn_803ACFC0 progress Retained reviewable target-only edits for fn_803ACFC0. Final measured score is 96.52336%, improved from the initial 90.63551% baseline and from the intermediate 96.42991% state. title: Worker progress for grMuteCity_801F1328
text: src/melee/gr/grmutecity.c main/melee/gr/grmutecity grMuteCity_801F1328 progress Retained a validated rewrite of grMuteCity_801F1328 that raises the local checkdiff score from the observed working-tree baseline 79.16092% to 98.43678%. The main gains came from matching the target's counted insertion-sort loop shape, removing the target's stale PAD_STACK, splitting wrapped-index computation into sum/idx, and avoiding cached car pointers in the pairwise car-flag loop so MWCC recomputes grMc_8049F4B8[arr[i]]/arr[idx] like the target. The target function addresses grMc_8049F4B8 entries through the grMc_8049F440 BSS base plus idx * 0x2C + 0x78; the retained direct grMc_8049F4B8[arr[*]] expressions preserve that addressing pattern. The first sort loop wants a counted inner loop with mtctr/bdnz and a pointer pre-decrement swap shape; the original while (j > 0 && ...) form was too high-level for the target. The pairwise car loop wants repeated direct arr[i]/arr[idx] car lookups rather than cached my_car/other_car pointer locals. A separate sum local for i + j and an idx local for the wrapped value better matches the target's wrapped-index codegen. Remaining mismatches are now narrow register-allocation/load-order issues rather than broad control-flow or stack-frame mismatches.

ParticleScreenState::_pad4 starts at offset 0x54; _pad4[0xA + port*0xC] maps to PADStatus.err at 0x5E, 0x6A, 0x76, and 0x82. MWCC unrolls the four-iteration reset loop and keeps the 0x80000000 mask as a shift source, matching the target's srw-based reset-mask construction better than literal constant ORs. The explicit port scan gives the best branch shape found, but the compiler keeps port in r3 while the target uses r5 and also introduces an r3 pointer for later port checks. Splitting the button delta computation into xor, xC0 store, then and improves the suffix ordering substantially.
